NEW TAB: AMBIGUOUS INTENTIONS @@PB@@ you can play this game at parties, with your friends, lovers etc. Basically the idea is to confuse those who are trying to parse your actions, and trace them back to a single intention - intentionally perform actions with multiple possible motivations, OR actions with no specific motivation. In order to do so, you'll want to employ a (?) mechanism to randomize your intentions such as flipping a coin, or deciding based on the shape of the cloud. There will be no intention if/when others try to trace it back - there is no game you can play, you are not trying to accomplish any goal - wo, when people trying to understand what goal you might be trying to accomplish, they won't be able to find any. this can be tricky, as, if they are looking too hard for an intention, and are willing to believe in one without sufficient evidence, you'll probably find yourself playing in to whatever intention they've made up for you…But it won't be that big a deal.

Rules on the Periphery Moses got the 10 commandments because he couldn't remember them when he wasn't in the right mindset. We give ourselves rules when we know we won't always be in this mental state - "don't eat sugar, exercise everyday," when we are focused on our long term goals vs. wallowing in sadness "oh yes - my sensible self said this" and I trust her, even though I do not understand her currently" We can set rules for others who are too young to understand - don't pee in the pool - or don't have the same morals side note (?) -> we can refer back/get back to any mind set, by seeing one of the patterns we collected. pieces of evidence we picked up and trying to remember why it was important to us - drawer pull THEN: we can turn our pattern collecting habits into a rule and try to collect the patterns of a specific collector even if we don't know why ex. things that made me smile. Things that are beautiful. Things that I like about myself (or: things that made me angry. things that i don't like about other people - (these are in a third realm where we do not know if they'd be helpful/ they might be deceptively helpful

Anyway - the concept it is on the periphery of our thoughts, not in the middle, so when we use SET theory - it accesses that part of our brain, and if I do so kind of like the decision pipeline, because,: only at certain junctures do we even consider the "rules", and for this, we access the {patterns} that are on the periphery of our imagination.

But in high school, I got this strange bug that I don't think had bitten anyone else. Once I'd written the lyrics to kryptonite over and over again. I was starting to develop my own theories during my two fav. classes - art and science. I took the phrase instrumentalism and ran with it. I don't think I even did well in school cause I gave up paying attention - it was torture to try to learn something at an arbitrary pace, and I soon became lost, having given up altogether. Instead, I started writing this alternate "textbook" with my ideas. The project warped and transformed to a short treatise, to a full textbook by the time I'd gotten to college and transferred.

One day there was a storm. Lying on the mattress I had shoved under my bed frame, staring up at the mirror I had placed face down on the bed frame. How it struck me as a revelation I'll never understand - in all my minutes of pondering, evidently I had never considered simply up and leaving - making my dreams come true now - starting the business I wanted - devoting more than my extra moments to the textbook I wanted to write - to my theories - taking the background and making it the foreground.
